mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent in a monthly release, it signals labor market normalization, which reduces recession risk premiums and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 9.0 in a monthly release
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 9.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances (RSAFS) combined with stable industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong household funding capacity without supply chain bottlenecks, driving positive earnings expectations and SPX gains over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS and stable INDPRO changes predict negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent, subsequent FEDFUNDS increases signal aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite rising rates and elevated inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential monthly increases in housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and construction activity, which historically lead broader economic expansion and support corporate earnings, yielding positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases fail to predict positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls gaining more than 150,000 in a single month confirms robust labor demand, reducing macroeconomic uncertainty and driving positive SPX momentum over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60 percent of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 150,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside declining unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion successfully absorbing slack, which historically precedes equity outperformance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO increases and UNRATE declines predict negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5 percent annualized, contracting consumer credit amplifies financial stress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5 percent despite credit contraction.
